rulings.Case Title: CARL HILSZ VS VERA CORT, et a
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance With Agreement To Produce Documents, And For Monetary Sanctions Against Defts And Her Attorneys
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 7, 2014, line 6. PLAINTIFF CARL HILSZ'S Motion To Compel Compliance With Agreement To Produce Documents, And For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants And Her Attorneys. Granted. Within 15 days, Defendant shall serve further responses and produce responsive documents in a manner that complies with the provisions of the protective order, as follows: 1. Defendant shall produce monthly parking lot diagrams from January 1, 2007 to January 1, 2014 (84 months less the nine months already produce). If no additional diagrams exist, Defendant shall state that a diligent search and reasonable inquiry has been made, identifying what efforts have been made to locate them. 2. Defendant shall produce all parking leases for all 103 spaces from 2007 to the present, including parking lease provisions in commercial leases. Defendant may redact from the amount paid for the commercial lease and other information subject to the right of privacy (i.e. Social Security nos.) However, the amount and details regarding the parking leases are to be produced without redaction. 3. Defendant shall produce all checks or payments showing taxes paid to the City of San Francisco from 2007 to the present. The search shall include all files, which are in the possession custody or control of the defendant, including bank records which may be in the current custody of the defendant's bank(s) and which can be obtained by the defendant by requesting copies. 4. Defendant shall produce accounting records, including the book, legers, spreadsheets and other records which a business customarily keeps in their business operations showing the revenue and expenses of the business from 2007 to the present which specifically relate to the 103 parking spaces. Other accounting records that do not relate to the parking spaces need not be produced. 6. Defendant shall produce all tax returns from 2007 to the present. Of the approximately 28 tax returns, only 19 were produced, and Defendant shall produce the remaining tax returns. If there are no additional tax returns, Defendant shall state that a diligent search and reasonable inquiry have been made, identifying what efforts have been made to locate them. However, the search for the missing tax returns shall include all files, which are in the possession custody or control of the defendant, including records which may be in the current custody of the defendant's accountants, bookkeepers or tax preparers which can be obtained by the defendant by requesting copies. 7. Defendant may produce a privilege log that explains each redaction or withheld document in accordance with CCP 2031.240. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $ 1,800.00 are awarded in favor of Plaintiff Carl Hilsz and against Defendants to be paid within 30 days of notice of entry of order. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: TERRY WILLIAM IRELAND et al VS. GORDON & REES LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Evidentiary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, April 7, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT ROPERS, MAJESKI, KOHN & BENTLEY PC's motion For Evidentiary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Jeffrey Windsor, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Court Orders that: 1. Plaintiffs comply with the Court¿s prior 2 orders within 10 days of the hearing on this motion, including the payment of sanctions and production, without objection, of all documents responsive to RMKB¿s document demands; 2. Monetary sanctions of $8,500 for the costs and fees associated with this motion, assessed jointly and severally against Plaintiffs and their attorneys, and shall be paid no later than 10 days after the hearing on this motion; 3. This Court retains jurisdiction over this motion: Plaintiffs, and their attorneys, shall submit declarations of compliance to the Court no later than 10 days after the hearing of this motion, said declarations to confirm payment of the sanctions ordered herein, production of documents and the details of Plaintiffs diligent search and reasonable inquiry; and 4. If Plaintiffs fail to comply with para. 3, then: a. Plaintiffs are precluded from offering, referring to, or otherwise using at trial any evidence that the underlying judgment in the TAT litigation was a substantial factor in causing Plaintiffs any damage; and b. Plaintiffs are precluded from offering, referring to, or otherwise using at trial any evidence that Defendants RMKB and/or Sue Handelman either misrepresented material facts or opinions, or failed to disclose material facts or opinions; and c. Plaintiff are precluded from offering, referring to, or otherwise using at trial any evidence that Plaintiff David Feldman communicated with any other person who was a party to the TAT litigation, including his co-defendants in that case; and d. Plaintiffs are precluded from offering, referring to, or otherwise using at trial or for any other purpose any document not produced on January 2, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jeff@windsor-plc.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: MISHA NASLEDOV VS. HOUSE OF AIR, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ; Or, In The Alternative, Mtn For Summ Adjudication (Ntc)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 7, 2014, line 10. DEFENDANT HOUSE OF AIR'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT; Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication. Granted as to House of Air's Motion for Summary judgment. The defendant has demonstrated a complete defense exists as to all causes of action. The signed waiver of liability (Crowley Decl., Ex. C.) is a complete defense as to the second, third, fourth, and fifth causes of action. The signed release is clear, unambiguous, and explicit in releasing all claims and causes of action against the Defendant, including any claims arising from Defendant's own negligence. The evidence shows that Plaintiff understood the language and effect of the release. Thus, summary judgment is granted as to Plaintiff's second cause of action for products negligence, third cause of action for breach of express warranty, fourth cause of action for breach of implied warranty, and fifth cause of action for ordinary negligence. While the release is ineffective to bar Plaintiff's first cause of action for strict product liability, Defendant has adequately demonstrated that Plaintiff cannot establish an element, where a condition precedent to maintaining a strict liability claim is that the primary objective was to acquire ownership or use of a product, and not to obtain a service. (See Westlye v. Look Sports, (1993) 17 Cal.App.4th 1715, 1747; Ferrari v. Grand Canyon Dories (1995) 32 Cal.App.4th 248, 258.) The evidence shows that Defendant provides additional services, over and above the mere use of trampoline equipment (see Ontiveros v. 24 Hour Fitness Corp, (2008) 169 Cal.App.4th 424, 434.) Thus, summary judgment is proper as to Plaintiff's first cause of action for strict liability. While the release is ineffective to a claim of gross negligence, Defendant has demonstrated a complete defense in the assumption of risk doctrine. Defendant's arrangement of trampolines cannot be said to be "totally outside the range of ordinary activity" (see Freeman v. Hale, (1994) 30 Cal. App. 4th 1388, 1394), in light of the inherent risks of the sport of trampolines. The burden then shifted to Plaintiff to show "a want of even scant care" or "an extreme departure from ordinary conduct" (Wallace v. Busch Entm't Corp., 837 F. Supp. 2d 1093, 1101 (S.D. Cal. 2011).). The evidence does not show that Defendant's conduct rose to the level of "scant care" or an "extreme departure from ordinary conduct." Thus, summary judgment as to Plaintiff's sixth cause of action for gross negligence is granted. Plaintiff concedes that no evidence of misrepresentation has been discovered. (P's Opp, 2: 15-16.) Defendant's motion for summary judgment as to the seventh cause of action for intentional misrepresentation, and the eighth cause of action for negligent misrepresentation, is granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: GUSTAVO RIOS VS. CERCLE DE L'UNION, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION DOING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Responses To Form Interoggs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, April 7, 2014, Line 3, DEFENDANT CERCLE DE L'UNION, DBA THE FRENCH CLUB'S (THE FRENCH) Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interoggatories Pro Tem Judge Jeffrey Windsor, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. The motion is not opposed. Plaintiff shall produce verified responses, without objections, within 10 days of the date of the Order. Plaintiff shall also produce documents responsive to Defendant¿s requests within 10 days of the date of the Order. Plaintiffs request for monetary sanctions is Granted in the amount of $367.50. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jeff@windsor-plc.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: RUSLAN VORONETSKY VS. CAL WOOD FLOORING SUPPLY, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 7, 2014, line 16. DEFENDANT CAL WOOD FLOORING SUPPLY, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained with 20 days leave to amend. (CCP 430.10(e).) Plaintiff is granted leave to amend to allege facts establishing that the first through seventh causes of action arise from conduct other than Defendant failing to file a worker's compensation claim. Plaintiff is further granted leave to amend to allege facts establishing Plaintiff's injuries are a "disability," as defined by FEHA. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend to allege facts establishing that Defendant knew about Plaintiff's disability and did not respond to requests for accommodation. Plaintiff is also given leave to amend the harassment cause of action to plead facts establishing that Defendant's "actions [were] outside the scope of job duties which are not of a type necessary to business and personnel management." (Reno v. Baird (1998) 18 Cal.4th 640, 647.) Lastly, Plaintiff is given leave to amend to plead facts establishing the causation element of the retaliation cause of action. (Yanowitz v. L'Oreal USA, Inc. (2005) 36 Cal.4th 1028, 1042.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MARGA ROSAS VS. LEVY, ERLANGER AND COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA BUSINESS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Compel Binding Arb
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 7, 2014, line 18. DEFENDANTS LEVY, ERLANGER AND COMPANY, DAVID LEVY, WILLIAM ERLANGER Petition To Compel Binding Arbitration. Defendants' motion to compel arbitration and stay litigation is granted pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 1281.2. Plaintiff and Defendants formed a valid arbitration agreement and the agreement covers Plaintiff's dispute with Defendants. The Agreement is not unconscionable: (1) Plaintiff offers no evidence that the Defendants presented the Agreement to the Plaintiff on a take it or leave it basis or that signing the Agreement was a condition of employment; (2) the language of the Agreement is straightforward; (3) the arbitration provision was on the 3rd page of a 4 page document and the word "arbitration" is in bold; and (4) the AAA rules incorporated into the Agreement are fair. Defendants were not required to provide a copy of the AAA rules with the Agreement. The arbitration provision in the Agreement covers Plaintiff's dispute with the Defendants; all of Plaintiff's claims arise from her employment with the Defendants and the Agreement defined the scope of Plaintiff's employment. Defendants attempted to negotiate a mutually agreeable arbitrator with the Plaintiff, but the Plaintiff demanded the Defendants to agree only to Plaintiff's proposed arbitrators before filing the Complaint. Finally, Defendants are the proper parties to the arbitration provision because Levy & Company is presently known as Levy, Erlanger and Company and Plaintiff's Complaint alleges that Plaintiff "was hired by Defendant Levy and Company (presently Levy, Erlanger and Company) . . . ." (Erlanger decl. 1:24-25; Complaint 14.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: SF HOTEL 447, LP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED PARTNERSHIP VS. T R & A, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Contractual Arbitration To Stay Action
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 7, 2014, line 19. DEFENDANT T R & A, INC.'S Petition To Compel Contractual Arbitration To Stay Action. Granted. This action is stayed pending the completion of arbitration. Plaintiff is judicially estopped from arguing that it was not a party to the Consulting Agreement. In its complaint, Plaintiff explicitly states that it entered into the Consulting Agreement with Defendant and attaches the Consulting Agreement with the arbitration clause as Exhibit A. (Complaint, par. 6; see Valerio v. Andrew Youngquist Construction (2002) 103 Cal.App.4th 1264, 1271.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
